diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Uremic encephalopathy with bilateral basal ganglia lesions
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with hypomimia, shuffling gait, bradykinesia, and resting tremor in both hands, along with elevated blood glucose, urea nitrogen, and serum creatinine levels. Brain MRI showed symmetrical basal ganglia lesions characterized by hypointensity on T1-weighted image, and hyperintensity on T2-weighted and FLAIR images. The symptoms improved with intensive hemodialysis, supporting the diagnosis of uremic encephalopathy.
